notes: Cardinal number 'one'. Confirmed identically across Wikivoyage's and Wikitravel's Sranan phrasebooks, Wiktionary/kaikki.org's Sranan Tongo dictionary, Glosbe (en/srn 'one'), and languagesandnumbers.com. Wiktionary notes 'wan' also doubles as the indefinite article 'a/an' before a singular noun — same word, different grammatical function.

Cardinal number 'two', from English 'two'. Confirmed identically in Wikivoyage's and Wikitravel's Sranan phrasebooks, Wiktionary/kaikki.org, and languagesandnumbers.com. Note: Wiktionary lists a separate, unrelated homograph 'tu' meaning 'too/also' (from English 'too') — distinct etymology, same spelling.
Cardinal number 'three'. Confirmed identically in Wikivoyage's and Wikitravel's Sranan phrasebooks, Wiktionary/kaikki.org (etymology: Dutch 'drie' or English 'three'), and languagesandnumbers.com.
Cardinal number 'four', from English 'four'. Confirmed identically in Wikivoyage's and Wikitravel's Sranan phrasebooks, Wiktionary/kaikki.org, and languagesandnumbers.com.

Cardinal number 'five', from Dutch 'vijf'/English 'five'. Confirmed as 'feifi' in Wikivoyage's phrasebook, Wiktionary/kaikki.org, and languagesandnumbers.com. Wikitravel's phrasebook gives the alternate spelling 'feyfi' (Wiktionary lists this as a valid alt form, not proscribed) — using the more common 'feifi' spelling here.
Cardinal number 'six', from English 'six'. Confirmed identically (no spelling variants found) in Wikivoyage's and Wikitravel's Sranan phrasebooks, Wiktionary/kaikki.org, and languagesandnumbers.com.

Cardinal number 'seven', from English 'seven'. Confirmed as 'seibi' in Wikivoyage's phrasebook, Wiktionary/kaikki.org, and languagesandnumbers.com. Wikitravel's phrasebook gives the alternate spelling 'seybi' (Wiktionary lists this as a valid alt form) — using the more common 'seibi' spelling here.

Cardinal number 'eight', from English 'eight'. Confirmed as 'aiti' in Wikivoyage's phrasebook, Wiktionary/kaikki.org, and languagesandnumbers.com. Wikitravel's phrasebook gives 'ayti', which Wiktionary explicitly flags as a proscribed (discouraged) alternate spelling — deliberately using the standard 'aiti' form here rather than Wikitravel's.

Cardinal number 'nine', borrowed from Dutch 'negen'. Confirmed as 'neigi' in Wikivoyage's phrasebook, Wiktionary/kaikki.org, and languagesandnumbers.com. Wikitravel's phrasebook gives the alternate spelling 'neygi' (Wiktionary lists this as a valid alt form) — using the more common 'neigi' spelling here.

Cardinal number 'ten'. Confirmed identically (no spelling variants found) in Wikivoyage's and Wikitravel's Sranan phrasebooks, Wiktionary/kaikki.org, Glosbe (en/srn 'ten' → 'tin', described as 'the cardinal number occurring after nine and before eleven'), and languagesandnumbers.com.

Cardinal number 'eleven'. Source: Koen Kamphuijs's 'Teri ini Sranantongo' (http://koenkamphuijs.nl/sranantongo/sranater.html) — single-sourced, not yet cross-checked against a second independent source.

Cardinal number 'twelve'. Source: Koen Kamphuijs's 'Teri ini Sranantongo' (http://koenkamphuijs.nl/sranantongo/sranater.html) — single-sourced, not yet cross-checked against a second independent source.

Cardinal number 'thirteen', literally 'ten-three' (tin + dri). Source: Koen Kamphuijs's 'Teri ini Sranantongo' (http://koenkamphuijs.nl/sranantongo/sranater.html) — single-sourced, not yet cross-checked against a second independent source.

Cardinal number 'fourteen', literally 'ten-four' (tin + fo). The site gives this as 'tinafo, etc.', implying the tin+digit pattern continues through nineteen, but only 13 and 14 are spelled out explicitly — 15–19 are deliberately not authored here rather than extrapolated. Source: Koen Kamphuijs's 'Teri ini Sranantongo' (http://koenkamphuijs.nl/sranantongo/sranater.html) — single-sourced, not yet cross-checked against a second independent source.

Cardinal number 'twenty', literally 'two-tens' (tu + tenti). Source: Koen Kamphuijs's 'Teri ini Sranantongo' (http://koenkamphuijs.nl/sranantongo/sranater.html) — single-sourced, not yet cross-checked against a second independent source.

Cardinal number 'thirty', literally 'three-tens' (dri + tenti). Source: Koen Kamphuijs's 'Teri ini Sranantongo' (http://koenkamphuijs.nl/sranantongo/sranater.html) — single-sourced, not yet cross-checked against a second independent source.

Cardinal number 'forty', literally 'four-tens' (fo + tenti). Source: Koen Kamphuijs's 'Teri ini Sranantongo' (http://koenkamphuijs.nl/sranantongo/sranater.html) — single-sourced, not yet cross-checked against a second independent source.

Cardinal number 'fifty', literally 'five-tens' (feifi + tenti). Spelled 'feyfitenti' on the source site; normalized to this repo's existing 'feifi' spelling (see srn-num-feifi) for consistency. Source: Koen Kamphuijs's 'Teri ini Sranantongo' (http://koenkamphuijs.nl/sranantongo/sranater.html) — single-sourced, not yet cross-checked against a second independent source.

Cardinal number 'sixty', literally 'six-tens' (siksi + tenti). Source: Koen Kamphuijs's 'Teri ini Sranantongo' (http://koenkamphuijs.nl/sranantongo/sranater.html) — single-sourced, not yet cross-checked against a second independent source.

Cardinal number 'seventy', literally 'seven-tens' (seibi + tenti). Spelled 'seybitenti' on the source site; normalized to this repo's existing 'seibi' spelling (see srn-num-seibi) for consistency. Source: Koen Kamphuijs's 'Teri ini Sranantongo' (http://koenkamphuijs.nl/sranantongo/sranater.html) — single-sourced, not yet cross-checked against a second independent source.

Cardinal number 'eighty', literally 'eight-tens' (aiti + tenti). Spelled 'aytitenti' on the source site; normalized to this repo's existing 'aiti' spelling (see srn-num-aiti) for consistency. Source: Koen Kamphuijs's 'Teri ini Sranantongo' (http://koenkamphuijs.nl/sranantongo/sranater.html) — single-sourced, not yet cross-checked against a second independent source.

Cardinal number 'ninety', literally 'nine-tens' (neigi + tenti). Spelled 'neygitenti' on the source site; normalized to this repo's existing 'neigi' spelling (see srn-num-neigi) for consistency. Source: Koen Kamphuijs's 'Teri ini Sranantongo' (http://koenkamphuijs.nl/sranantongo/sranater.html) — single-sourced, not yet cross-checked against a second independent source.

Cardinal number 'one hundred', literally 'one hundred' (wan + ondro). The site also gives the alternate spelling 'wan hondro'. Source: Koen Kamphuijs's 'Teri ini Sranantongo' (http://koenkamphuijs.nl/sranantongo/sranater.html) — single-sourced, not yet cross-checked against a second independent source.

Cardinal number 'one thousand'. Source: Koen Kamphuijs's 'Teri ini Sranantongo' (http://koenkamphuijs.nl/sranantongo/sranater.html) — single-sourced, not yet cross-checked against a second independent source.

Cardinal number 'one million'. Source: Koen Kamphuijs's 'Teri ini Sranantongo' (http://koenkamphuijs.nl/sranantongo/sranater.html) — single-sourced, not yet cross-checked against a second independent source.
